children.text: the supplying country and receiving country have reached agreement to place all such equipment, materials, or technology, upon delivery, under multilateral auspices and management when available; and

the recipient country has entered into an agreement with the International Atomic Energy Agency to place all such equipment, materials, technology, and all nuclear fuel and facilities in such country under the safeguards system of such Agency.

children.heading: Certification by President of necessity of continued assistance; disapproval by Congress
children.label: b Certification by President of necessity of continued assistance; disapproval by Congress
A
children.text: the termination of such assistance would have a serious adverse effect on vital United States interests; and

children.label: B
children.text: he has received reliable assurances that the country in question will not acquire or develop nuclear weapons or assist other nations in doing so.

children.label: A
children.text: A certification under paragraph (1) of this subsection shall take effect on the date on which the certification is received by the Congress. However, if, within thirty calendar days after receiving this certification, the Congress enacts a joint resolution stating in substance that the Congress disapproves the furnishing of assistance pursuant to the certification, then upon the enactment of that resolution the certification shall cease to be effective and all deliveries of assistance furnished under the authority of that certification shall be suspended immediately.

children.label: B
children.text: Any joint resolution under this paragraph shall be considered in the Senate in accordance with the provisions of section 601(b) of the International Security Assistance and Arms Export Control Act of 1976.
